newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The last event was the Q3 FY2026 print on May 4 (revenue +12% to $49.2M but a small net loss vs. prior-year profit on margin/mix), plus a routine $0.15 quarterly dividend. The +9% move since June 9 is flow/noise with no AI-campus security demand catalyst.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new latent AI-facility datapoint since June 9. Q3 (May 4) was recurring-revenue-driven physical security, not compute-campus access control; the +9% bounce is flow. The strict-screen thesis is unchanged, so hold where the prior down-move left it.
verdicts.bull: Recurring service revenue (Starlink fire radios, MVP cloud access, copper-to-cellular) grows margin; could serve high-security edge sites.
verdicts.neutral: Solid recurring-revenue security vendor, but no demonstrated AI-campus pull-through.
verdicts.bear: Q3 swung to a net loss; physical security has no real AI-node criticality, so it does not belong high on a strict latent screen.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Recurring-revenue mix and any data-center access-control wins
verdicts.primaryRisk: No AI-facility demand materializes; remains a generic security name
